weekly_summary: Low-volume foundation week to establish aerobic base and assess current fitness. Recent activity shows minimal training load (1 hour over 14 days), so we rebuild gradually with easy runs and strategic rest.

days.title: Full Recovery
days.description: Complete rest day. Focus on sleep, hydration, and nutrition to prepare for training block.
days.notes: Note: Evening run already logged (3.62 km). If this run already occurred, count it as an easy run instead and replace Monday with rest.

days.title: Easy Aerobic Base
days.description: Conversational pace, fully aerobic effort. Should be able to speak full sentences comfortably. Focus on relaxed cadence (160-170 rpm).
days.notes: Run by feel/perceived effort. Target pace: 6:30/km. If HR monitor available, aim for 65-75% max HR.

days.title: Active Recovery
days.description: Rest day or optional 20-minute easy walk (Z1). Prioritize mobility work, stretching, and foam rolling.
days.notes: Monitor fatigue signals. Given low recent volume, additional recovery is warranted.

days.title: Easy Aerobic Base
days.description: Same effort as Tuesday—conversational, relaxed, fully sustainable. Build aerobic capacity without fatigue accumulation.
days.notes: Note: Evening run already logged (6.31 km on this date). Integrate or adjust total accordingly. Pace: ~6:30/km.

days.title: Full Recovery
days.description: Complete rest day before the weekend long run. Sleep and nutrition are critical.
days.notes: Hydrate well. Prepare mentally for Saturday long run.

days.title: Foundation Long Run
days.description: Extended easy run to build aerobic engine and mental toughness. Conversational pace throughout. Take water and fuel if needed after 60 minutes.
days.notes: Pace: ~6:30/km. Start conservatively—finish strong. This is base-building long run, not race-pace effort.

days.title: Full Recovery
days.description: Complete rest and recovery. Reflect on week, prepare nutrition and hydration for next week.
days.notes: Use this day for meal prep, sleep, and light mobility work if desired.

coaching_notes: Athlete shows very low recent training volume (1 hour over 14 days), indicating either recent break or off-season. Week 1 prioritizes gradual re-entry with 42.5 km total volume (~5.5 hours), distributed across 4 running days with 3 rest days. All runs are easy-zone to rebuild aerobic base safely and avoid injury. No high-intensity work yet—polarized approach begins with strong aerobic foundation. The athlete has 7.0-hour weekly budget; this plan uses ~5.5 hours, leaving buffer for adaptation or additional easy volume if recovery permits. Monitor for excessive fatigue or soreness; if present, reduce long run to 10 km next week.
recovery_flags: Low recent training volume suggests detraining—progress conservatively over next 2-3 weeks.
No HR data available—use perceived effort and pace targets as primary guides.
Long run (14 km) is modest but intentional given recent inactivity; listen to body.
next_week_preview: Week 2 will maintain similar easy volume (~45 km) with introduction of one tempo or threshold session to begin polarization. Long run increases to 16 km. Continue monitoring HRV and fatigue before advancing intensity.
